text: Concurrent strength and endurance training produces a small but significant improvement in running economy, with an average reduction in oxygen cost of approximately 4%.
Explosive strength training (plyometrics, jump training) is the most effective concurrent training type for improving running economy.
Heavy weight training is effective for improving running economy in endurance runners.
Isometric strength training does not produce a statistically significant improvement in running economy.
Longer duration concurrent training programs produce larger improvements in running economy than shorter programs.
Running economy improvements from concurrent strength training can be observed within a few weeks, but long-term programs are needed for the largest possible gains.
